items.title: Keep track of today's sharp drop in petrol prices around the country

children.content: The federal government's cut to fuel excise has almost immediately flowed through to petrol prices at the pump, according to ABC tracking of fuel price data nationwide.

articles.title: Fuel prices begin to fall at petrol stations — quicker than expected

children.content: Consumers were warned the fuel excise cut might take weeks to be passed on to retailers, but prices have already begun to fall, NRMA says.

image.altText: petrol prices

articles.title: Australia assessing ways to help Pacific nations that 'basically run on diesel'

children.content: Pacific nations are not yet suffering large-scale petrol shortages but are already starting to grapple with the economic impacts of the war, with fuel price hikes threatening to drive up inflation.

image.altText: Close up of BP petrol pump going into a car.

items.title: 'Ridiculous': How Australians are coping with sky-high fuel prices

children.content: The ABC asked how you were coping with fuel prices and here's what we heard back.

image.altText: A composite image of two men and two women.

items.title: National addresses are rare and historic. Now, Albanese will make another

children.content: Pandemics, wars, and landmark moments have been immortalised in addresses to the nation. Tonight, Anthony Albanese will be one of the few prime ministers to employ the form.

image.altText: Tight shot of Morrison is staring straight ahead wearing a mask with the Australian flag on it.

items.title: PM to urge Australians to 'play their part' in Iran response

children.content: The prime minister's office says Anthony Albanese will address the nation on TV tonight, urging Australians to save fuel for areas and industries that "most need it".

image.altText: A man in a suit looks across the camera with an australian flag in the background.

items.title: Anthropic boss on copyright, job losses and inevitable tax in AI future

children.content: The AI company's billionaire boss says he is not trying to convince Australia to change its mind on protecting the copyright of artists.

image.altText: A man and a woman sit on a stage at a forum with a crowd underneath.

images.altText: A man wearing a helmet and flak jacket with the word PRESS on it looks at the camera with building rubble behind him

items.title: Former Sydney University academic appears in Iranian propaganda video

children.content: An Australian academic has appeared in an Iranian propaganda video supporting a toll system imposed by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps on foreign merchant shipping.

items.title: More than 290,000 visits to Daniel's Law website in first three months

children.content: Queensland's first public child sex offender register, listing reportable offenders who have failed to comply with their reporting obligations or whose whereabouts are unknown, has been praised for protecting children.

image.altText: Profile of a young boy.

items.title: Dezi Freeman investigation to probe January bushfires 1km from hiding spot

children.content: Victoria Police have officially confirmed that the person shot dead by officers at a property in Victoria's north-east on Monday morning was fugitive Dezi Freeman.

image.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: 'Worse than COVID': How regional shops are feeling the economic pinch

children.content: Regional businesses buckle under rising costs, saying the economic situation is even worse than during COVID.

image.altText: A man with a white beard and a flat-cap in the doorway of a bookstore.

items.title: Arnold carried in jubilant scenes after Iraq qualifies for World Cup

children.content: Former Socceroos coach Graham Arnold has guided Iraq to their first FIFA World Cup appearance in 40 years with a playoff win over Bolivia.

gallery.altText: Iraq's coach Graham Arnold carried by players after qualifying for FIFA World Cup, massive crowd waiving flags

thumbnail.altText: Iraq's coach Graham Arnold carried by players after qualifying for FIFA World Cup, massive crowd waiving flags

items.title: Australia's solar panel waste issue is growing in size year-on-year

children.content: Australians are expected to throw away 90,000 tonnes of solar panel waste each year by 2030, a federal government hearing has been told.

image.altText: Four rows of dark solar panels on a silver roof.

items.title: Dire future of superb fairy-wren could mean catastrophe for other species

children.content: The researchers said the superb fairy-wren would likely go extinct within 30-40 years unless action was taken to stop greenhouse gas emissions causing climate change.

image.altText: A small blue-headed wren sitting in a tree.

items.title: Lawsuit filed against Taylor Swift over The Life of a Showgirl album

children.content: Las Vegas performer Maren Wade says the name and imagery of Taylor Swift's latest album are too close to those of her show, Confessions of a Showgirl.

image.altText: Taylor Swift poses in a body of water in a glittering silver corset

items.title: Bluey's lead composer on helping kids fall in love with music

children.content: A new orchestral Bluey album is out. Lead composer Joff Bush talks about classical music, storytelling and inspiring kids to love music.

image.altText: A man in a colourful shirt plays the melodica

items.title: How this Aussie went from TAFE to the head of Sony character animation

children.content: Daniel Pozo has had a hand in Sony hits like Spiderman: Across the Spider-Verse and Hotel Transylvania, but GOAT is his first project in the driver's seat.

gallery.altText: a group of anthropomorphic animals pose for a photo on a smartphone

items.title: New mine owner claims he's faced threats over removing bikie shareholders

children.content: The Tasmanian government has taken steps to revoke the operating licence of one of the state's biggest mining operations, after being warned it was allegedly used as a "vehicle for money laundering" and former owners were associated with underworld figures.

gallery.altText: A graphic of a mining site and a dark figure of a man in a bike helmet.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic of a mining site and a dark figure of a man in a bike helmet.

items.title: WA government to use emergency powers amid fuel crisis

children.content: The Western Australian government will use emergency powers to force fuel companies to provide information about their supply chains in an effort to keep fuel flowing through the state.

image.altText: WA Premier Roger Cook speaks to reporters

items.title: Share markets energised as they price in end to Iran war, but there's a catch

children.content: If you had woken up today and heard someone say Wall Street's tech sector rose more than 3 per cent overnight, you would expect it to be promptly followed by "April Fools' Day".

image.altText: A trader looks up at a chart on his computer screen while working on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange.

items.title: 'We are an expert': How Lebanon's airport keeps running despite Israeli strikes

children.content: Beirut-Rafic Hariri Airport, Lebanon's gateway to the world, is still operating even as Israel bombards its surroundings.

image.altText: An airport hall with lined floor, seats, a glass ceiling, and no people around

items.title: Fears for stingray populations as algal bloom moves north

children.content: Evidence of South Australia's harmful algal bloom has been detected in previously untouched parts of the Spencer Gulf days after rays were seen gathering in shallow waters.

image.altText: A dead stingray lying in a small puddle among rocks, with its white belly facing up

items.title: Cultural compensation replaces fine in 'rare' illegal logging ruling

children.content: Traditional owners say a $450,000 fine will not replace the six centuries-old giant trees illegally felled by NSW Forestry Corporation.

gallery.altText: An aerial view of a forest with a river running through it and falling down a waterfall.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial view of a forest with a river running through it and falling down a waterfall.

items.title: Spurs fans protest over new manager as post-Postecoglou woes deepen

children.content: Tottenham fan groups voice opposition to newly appointed manager Roberto De Zerbi over his previous public support for Mason Greenwood, a player who faced charges of attempted rape and assault.

image.altText: Roberto De Zerbi sits on the bench and looks to his left

items.title: Socceroos headliners shine, but will the experimentation pay off at World Cup?

children.content: The Socceroos are done with experimenting, but now need to find the winning formula with the World Cup quickly approaching.

items.title: 'Nothing's out here' Vodafone ad angers rural Australians

items.title: These old sugarcane farms could be turned into rainforest again

children.content: A man and woman will face court after allegedly stealing hundreds of Lego sets from retail outlets across metropolitan Adelaide.

items.title: Council monitor appointed after character reference saga embroils mayor

children.content: Wyndham City Council's Preet Singh wrote a character reference for Kashyap Patel, who in 2024 was convicted of grooming and sexually assaulting a 15-year-old girl.

items.title: American journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad

children.content: Police in Iraq say they are searching for an American journalist who has been kidnapped in Baghdad, possibly by an Iranian-backed militia group.

items.title: Meet the Artemis II crew who are heading to the Moon

items.title: Trump tells allies 'go get your own oil' from Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The US president again attacks America's allies over their reluctance to get involved in the Iran war, warning them the "US won't be there to help you anymore, just like you weren't there for us".

items.title: Could I be using E10 in my car?

children.content: Australia's leading motoring organisation is urging drivers to fill up with E10 fuel in an effort to conserve national fuel supplies. But what exactly is E10? And could it help ease the current fuel crisis?

image.altText: A man holds a petrol bowser while filling up his car.

items.title: Shein and Kmart accused of copying Queensland retailer's designs

children.content: Queensland business Sabo Skirt has claimed Shein, Kmart and numerous other retailers and wholesalers have copied its clothing patterns, prints and trademarks on "lower quality" garments in a federal court claim.

items.title: One of Australia's biggest 'giveaway' clubs has its licence cancelled

children.content: RS Rewards claims to have given away more than $10 million worth of cars, houses and cash. But its licence has just been cancelled.

children.content: There is a significant divide between property price growth in major cities as buyers weigh up interest rate hikes and geopolitical tensions.
